summary: Web Console Proxy and Monitor Type Switching - All tests passed. Backend: 13/13 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All UI features verified working. Web console modal opens with loading state, title bar, security badge, and close button. Monitor type switching works with toast notifications.

minor.issue: Fixed: audit_logger.info() was incorrect - AuditLogger doesn't have info method. Changed to logging.getLogger('audit').info()

backend_api_tests: POST /api/connector/web-proxy/request creates proxy request for managed device (192.168.1.3)
POST /api/connector/web-proxy/request returns 403 for non-managed device IP (10.99.99.99)
GET /api/connector/web-proxy/pending with X-API-Key returns pending requests
POST /api/connector/web-proxy/response with X-API-Key accepts HTML response
GET /api/connector/web-proxy/response/{request_id} returns completed response with HTML body
Viewer role cannot create web proxy request (403 Insufficient permissions)
Operator role can create web proxy request (200 OK)
Monitor type change persists in device poll status
frontend_ui_tests: Connettori page loads with client groups
Monitor icon (web console button) visible next to each device
Clicking monitor icon opens web console modal
Web console modal has title bar with device info (WebConsole — 192.168.1.2:80)
Web console modal shows security badge 'Proxy sicuro'
Web console modal has close button (X)
Web console modal shows loading state 'In attesa del connettore...'
Web console modal shows connection path 'SOC → Backend → Connettore → IP:port'
Monitor type badge (SNMP/PING) visible next to each device
Clicking monitor type badge switches between SNMP and Ping+HTTP
Toast notification confirms monitor type change '192.168.1.2 cambiato a Ping+HTTP'

seed_data_creation: Used existing devices from previous iterations: 192.168.1.2 (HPEOfficeConnect Switch), 192.168.1.3 (NETGEAR GS110EMX). Test users (viewer, operator) created and cleaned up during tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Web Console Proxy feature fully verified. Backend endpoints: web-proxy/request (creates pending request, requires admin/operator role, validates device is managed), web-proxy/pending (connector polls with API key), web-proxy/response (connector submits HTML), web-proxy/response/{id} (frontend gets completed response). Monitor type switching: PUT /api/connector/device-poll-status/{ip}/monitor-type changes between snmp and ping. Frontend: Connettori page shows monitor icon next to each device, clicking opens modal with loading state, title bar, security badge, close button. Monitor type badge is clickable to switch types.

feature_verification.feature: Web Console Proxy and Monitor Type Switching

verification_results.web_proxy_request_endpoint: POST /api/connector/web-proxy/request creates pending request with security checks - VERIFIED
verification_results.web_proxy_pending_endpoint: GET /api/connector/web-proxy/pending returns pending requests with API key - VERIFIED
verification_results.monitor_type_endpoint: PUT /api/connector/device-poll-status/{ip}/monitor-type changes device monitor type - VERIFIED
verification_results.security_viewer_blocked: Viewer role cannot create web proxy requests (403) - VERIFIED
verification_results.frontend_monitor_icon: Monitor icon visible next to each device - VERIFIED
verification_results.frontend_web_console_modal: Modal opens with title bar, device info, security badge, close button - VERIFIED
verification_results.frontend_loading_state: Loading state shows 'In attesa del connettore...' - VERIFIED
verification_results.frontend_monitor_type_switch: Clicking type badge switches between SNMP and Ping+HTTP - VERIFIED

bug_fixed.issue: audit_logger.info() was called but AuditLogger class doesn't have an info method
bug_fixed.fix: Changed to logging.getLogger('audit').info() to use Python's standard logging module
